intake.topic_summary: Implement 9 missing subroutines in /Users/ghost/core/hive/tool/design_strategy_lint.hexa to satisfy proof-obligations of raw 109-117 (P1-P8 paradigm raws + raw 117 5-check meta-mandate). Each raw declared `enforce tool/design_strategy_lint.hexa#<slug>` but the file (frozen 2026-04-25, registered raw 46-76 only) lacked the 9 subroutines, leaving 9 outstanding proof-obligations. Implementation extends _strategies() membership (16→25), _slug_to_strategy() routing (16→25), _dispatch_tier3() dispatch (16→25), adds 9 fn _check_*() implementations, makes raw 117 cross-cutting via _flush_entry pre-dispatch hook, and adds 22 new selftest assertions. Output: 39541 → 59941 bytes; 839 → 1181 lines; selftest 'OK — 25 strategies' green; --strategy 5check-mandate against /Users/ghost/core/hive/.raw produces ZERO tier3[5check-mandate] hits on raw 109-117 (all 9 entries self-pass the 5-check gate). Per-paradigm tier3 hits == 0 across all 8 P-paradigms (raw entries themselves declare CONTRACT, not content fields, so subroutines correctly no-op). Pre-existing Tier 2 strategy-source/cross-repo-trawl-witness warnings retained (separate cluster discipline, out of scope).

alpha_absorbed.applied_to_design: absorbed motif = 'SSOT coverage noise-channel anchor witness'. Reframe: design_strategy_lint.hexa IS the SSOT coverage anchor for raw 109-117 — its proof-obligation field on each raw points to tool/design_strategy_lint.hexa#<slug>-selftest-PASS, making the lint itself the witness substrate. Implementation pattern follows existing 16 subroutines (raw 46-76) one-to-one, preserving the SSOT 'one slug → one subroutine → one selftest assertion' coverage invariant. The motif anchors the implementation discipline: each new fn must (a) live in the same file (single SSOT), (b) follow the existing return type [field, severity, reason], (c) gate enforcement on field presence to avoid false-positive on the contract-defining raw, (d) carry a selftest fixture proving the mapping works.

axis_a.rationale: WHAT — the 9 new fn _check_*() subroutines themselves: source-line position in design_strategy_lint.hexa, return-type contract, field-presence gating discipline. axis_a focuses on code emission correctness; axis_b focuses on selftest fixture coverage; axis_c focuses on raw 117 cross-cutting self-host fixpoint. β bias (mod 9 = 2) prioritized P2 raw 110 ordering, but axis_a remains the WHAT primary axis per user prompt 'concrete hexa code' explicit ask.

subroutines_added.checks: ≥3 adversarial-probe entries; each entry contains markers {attack_vector=, expected_failure_mode=, mitigation_path=, status=}
≥2 ablation-matrix rows (multi-component requirement); each row contains markers {component=, metric_with=, metric_without=, null_baseline=, marginal=}
expected-information-gain-bits paired with cost-units (raw 86 link); EIG must be non-negative integer
bayesian-aggregation contains markers {prior=, likelihood=, posterior=, decision-threshold=}
bisimulation-trace contains markers {N_steps=, state_old=, state_new=, observation_match=}
perturbation-experiment contains markers {perturbation_kind=, baseline_metric=, post_perturbation_metric=, classification=} AND classification value ∈ {fragile, robust, anti_fragile}

Cross-cutting on every design-* slug with status=new: (1) slug has no forbidden suffix {-via,-with,-api,-using}; (2) ≥2 cognitive-frameworks lines; (3) ≥3 realization-channels lines; (4) ≥1 counter-example line; (5) ≥1 falsifier line. Returns empty for non-design slugs OR status != 'new'.

axis_a.limit_class: HARD-bound (1:1 with raw 109-117 declaration; raw 118+ is next cycle)

axis_b.rationale: HOW the implementation is verified — 22 new selftest assertions extending the existing pattern. Each new subroutine gets (a) membership probe via _is_known_strategy, (b) at least one positive (PASS) case, (c) at least one negative (warn-emitting) case. Plus 3 representative _slug_to_strategy() routing assertions + 2 cross-cutting raw 117 skip-conditions (non-design slug + status≠new) + 1 forbidden-suffix detection probe. axis_b is HOW (verification surface); axis_a is WHAT (implementation surface); axis_c is WHY (self-host fixpoint witness). Pairwise correlation < 0.7 because axes test orthogonal properties.

selftest_assertions_added.form: _is_known_strategy('<name>') == true for each of 9 new strategy names; raw 109-117 each tagged
kvs with 3 well-formed adversarial-probe entries → 0 hits
kvs with 3 probes missing expected_failure_mode → ≥1 hit
ordering-only spec warns ≥2 markers; complete spec passes
(a) -via suffix flagged; (b) complete 5-fields pass; (c) sparse warns ≥4; (d) status=live skips; (e) non-design slug skips

axis_b.limit_class: MEDIUM-bound (assertions chosen to cover happy path + common failure mode per subroutine; exhaustive marker permutations deferred to fuzz-suite follow-up cycle)

axis_c.rationale: WHY — raw 117 5-check applied to ALL 9 newly-implemented subroutine targets (raw 109-117 entries themselves). raw 117 declares 'self-applied: raw 117 5-check ✓ ... raw 109-116 all simultaneously self-applied' (line 3213). This cycle makes that 1-step claim deterministically verifiable: hexa <tool> /Users/ghost/core/hive/.raw --strategy 5check-mandate produces ZERO tier3[5check-mandate] hits for ID range [109..117]. Each raw self-passes (a) genus-name no forbidden suffix, (b) ≥2 frameworks, (c) ≥3 channels, (d) ≥1 counter-example, (e) ≥1 falsifier. axis_c is the META-axis (the lint can lint itself + the rules it enforces); axis_a is OBJECT-axis (lint code); axis_b is VERIFY-axis (selftest). Triple is orthogonal across abstraction level.
axis_c.fixpoint_class: 1-step deterministic — N-step ω-saturation deferred to raw 118 (Q1 design-recursive-self-application-fixpoint already registered as next-layer paradigm)

fan_out.correlation_estimate: axis_a (code emission, fn-line-position) ⊥ axis_b (selftest fixture coverage) ⊥ axis_c (raw 117 self-host fixpoint witness across 9 raws). Pairwise correlation estimate: a×b ≤ 0.4 (selftest references the fns but tests their behavior, not their position); a×c ≤ 0.3 (self-host check operates on raw entry fields, not on fn source); b×c ≤ 0.5 (selftest has 5 fixtures for raw 117 cross-cutting, but those are HOW assertions, not WHY witness — witness lives in axis_c data block). All pairs < 0.7. Verified by inspection: no axis subsumes another.

axes_surfaced.name: subroutine-source-position

options_considered.option: A — append all 9 fns at end of file (after _check_paper_lineage, before _dispatch_tier3)
B — interleave each fn next to its closest existing relative (e.g., adversarial-co-evolution near _check_negative_oracle since both deal with adversarial framing)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: interleaving breaks the chronological registration order and forces re-numbering of selftest assertion order; raw 109-117 are a sequential block by registration date 2026-04-27 so block-append matches the .raw layout and minimizes diff churn

raw 109-117 each declare `enforce tool/design_strategy_lint.hexa#<slug>` — the path string is the SSOT contract; splitting forces .raw rewrites + breaks the single-SSOT-file invariant established in atlas raw 95 4-stub canonical pattern

options_considered.option: D — generate via macro/codegen at build time
options_considered.rejected_rationale: adds build-time dependency; raw 8 hexa-only-strict prefers hand-written direct code; codegen would also defeat the per-fn customization (each subroutine has different field-presence logic)

axes_surfaced.decision: Option A — block-append after _check_paper_lineage, retaining chronological raw 109→117 ordering inside the new block
axes_surfaced.rationale: Minimizes diff size, matches .raw registration order, preserves single-SSOT invariant, leaves room for raw 118+ to follow same append pattern. β qrng (mod 9 = 2) reinforced placing P2 raw 110 in second source position (matches stochastic outcome).

axes_surfaced.name: raw-117-cross-cutting-mechanism

options_considered.option: A — call _check_5check_mandate ONLY when slug=design-novelty-5check-mandate (single-strategy dispatch, like other 8)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: raw 117 contract scope: 'every NEW raw with category=design-strategy MUST satisfy ALL 5 sub-checks' — applying only to the contract-defining raw misses the entire point (the rule governs OTHER raws, not just itself)

options_considered.option: B — invoke _check_5check_mandate from inside _flush_entry BEFORE strategy-specific dispatch, gated on (status=new AND slug starts with design-)
C — fold the 5 checks INTO _flush_entry directly without a separate fn
options_considered.rejected_rationale: loses testability — _check_5check_mandate as a standalone fn allows direct selftest fixtures (5 currently); inlining forces e2e selftest only

options_considered.option: D — make 5-check a Tier 2 cluster-wide check alongside strategy-source/cross-repo-trawl-witness
options_considered.rejected_rationale: Tier 2 already has fixed witness fields (raw 47); mixing raw 117 into Tier 2 conflates separate cluster disciplines; Tier 3 dispatch with cross-cutting hook keeps concerns separate

axes_surfaced.decision: Option B — _check_5check_mandate is a normal Tier 3 fn (dispatched for slug=design-novelty-5check-mandate), AND _flush_entry invokes it cross-cuttingly for every other design-strategy slug with status=new (gated to avoid double-emit when filter==5check-mandate)
axes_surfaced.rationale: Preserves single-fn testability + matches raw 117's cross-cutting scope contract. Strategy_filter == '5check-mandate' triggers cross-cutting branch (covers all design-* raws); strategy_filter == '<other>' skips the cross-cut to keep filtered runs focused; strategy_filter == 'all' applies cross-cut everywhere AND strategy-specific dispatch.

axes_surfaced.name: field-presence-gating-discipline

options_considered.option: A — every subroutine returns hits[] ONLY when the strategy's content fields are at least partially declared (existing pattern, e.g., _check_paper_lineage)
B — every subroutine UNCONDITIONALLY emits hits[] when scanning raw with matching strategy slug
options_considered.rejected_rationale: false-positive on the contract-defining raws (109-116) — they declare scope/why/realization-channels but do NOT host content fields like adversarial-probe; unconditional emit would flood the lint output with 8 'missing required field' hits per .raw scan

options_considered.option: C — special-case the 8 contract-defining raws (109-116) with allowlist
options_considered.rejected_rationale: fragile — every new design-strategy raw registration would require updating the allowlist; pattern A's field-presence gating is allowlist-free and self-maintaining

options_considered.option: D — use raw status=new filter to gate (only enforce on new raws)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: raw 117 cross-cut already uses status=new, but per-paradigm subroutines are about field validation when CONTENT is declared, regardless of raw status — gating on status conflates concerns

axes_surfaced.decision: Option A — field-presence gating (early return when no content fields declared)
axes_surfaced.rationale: Matches existing 16-subroutine pattern. Verified: all 8 P-paradigm subroutines produce 0 tier3 hits when scanning hive/.raw (raw 109-116 declare contracts only). Subroutines fire only on artifacts that adopt the strategy and declare content fields.
